occurrence_sentence: DARPA's $2.1 Million Grant to Boost Quantum Algorithm Research at Lehigh University
DARPA awarded Lehigh University $2.1 million to study quantum algorithms.

Lehigh University will soon be on the front lines of quantum computing research with a $2.1 million grant from DARPA, according to [a university announcement.](https://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2020-03/lu-dg032020.php)
DARPA awarded Lehigh University $2.1 million to study quantum algorithms.

With support from a recently awarded $2,128,658 research grant from the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA), an international group led by industrial and systems engineering (ISE) faculty members Tamás Terlaky, Luis Zuluaga, and Boris Defourny will work on optimization algorithms in quantum computing.

interaction_phrase: who is also affiliated with Lehigh's Institute for Data, Intelligent Systems, and Computation
occurrence_sentence: Current quantum computers are about where silicone based computer chips were in the 1950s, said Terlaky, who is also affiliated with Lehigh's Institute for Data, Intelligent Systems, and Computation (I-DISC).
target_actor: Institute for Data, Intelligent Systems, and Computation (I-DISC)

occurrence_sentence: Additional researchers involved in the DARPA project include Giacomo Nannicini, IBM T.J. Watson Research Center; Stefan Wild, NAISE, Evanston, IL, and Argonne National Lab; Alain Sarlette, INRIA, Paris, France; Xiu Yang, ISE, Lehigh University and Monique Laurent, Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica( CWI), Amsterdam, Netherlands.
